meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn a proven client workflow to generate high-value project ideas using Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for freelancers.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. The secret to endless project ideas is building a repeatable client workflow that audits industry pain points. By using Antigravity to map business requirements and Claude Code to scaffold the MVP, you transform vague client needs into high-converting, billable software assets in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer’s greatest enemy. You have the technical skills to build anything, but you’re stuck in a loop of 'what should I build next?' while your billable hours slip away. Most developers treat projects as isolated events rather than a repeatable system, leading to burnout and inconsistent income.

It’s time to stop treating software development like art and start treating it like a supply chain. By integrating Antigravity for requirement intelligence and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can turn a client's half-baked thought into a production-ready roadmap before they even finish their first coffee.

steps.title: Identify High-Value Pain Points
steps.description: Use Antigravity to analyze a specific industry niche and extract the top three recurring operational bottlenecks that software can solve.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current operational bottlenecks for boutique fitness studios. Identify 3 high-impact features that would automate lead management and client retention.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'research fitness studio pain points and output a prioritized feature list'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Scope
steps.description: Define the minimum feature set that provides immediate ROI. Focus on one core workflow to minimize scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a technical project roadmap for a fitness lead management dashboard. Include data schema and primary API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project structure and database schema for a CRUD application focusing on lead management'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Application
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the initial boilerplate, ensuring professional standards and clean architecture from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React + Tailwind boilerplate with a focus on modular component design for a dashboard layout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'init project with Next.js, Tailwind, and ShadcnUI components'

steps.title: Automate Feature Implementation
steps.description: Iteratively build out features by feeding your Antigravity-generated specs directly into Claude Code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the logic for an automated email trigger based on lead status changes in the fitness dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a server action to handle lead status updates and trigger email notifications'

steps.title: Deployment and Client Handoff
steps.description: Finalize the build and package the code for easy deployment, ensuring the client has a clear path to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a documentation summary and deployment checklist for the client to host this application on Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'generate a README.md with setup instructions and deployment steps for a non-technical client'

pro_tips: Always map your database schema in Antigravity before touching a single line of code; it prevents 90% of refactoring headaches.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests alongside your code; clients pay 20% more for 'enterprise-grade' reliability.
Never build custom authentication; use Clerk or Auth0 and have Claude Code integrate it in seconds.
Keep your Antigravity prompts focused on 'business outcomes' rather than 'code structure' to get better architectural advice.
When a client asks for 'one more feature', use Antigravity to estimate the impact, then present it as an upsell, not a favor.
content.next_steps: After building your first project, create a 'Service Product' page on your portfolio site using the same workflow. Market the specific solution you just built to three companies in that niche.
content.conversion_block: Stop chasing clients and start building assets that sell themselves. With this system, you move from a developer to an architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
